baseline_dominance.justification: Attacks from rfc7662_advocate and spiffe_engineer confirm no baseline can replicate the combination of issuer-hiding + predicate evaluation over hidden attributes without a trusted third party. rfc7662 Attack 1: federation AS leaks to AS. Attack 2: PPID+DPoP cannot do predicate-hiding. Attack 3: AS policy filtering requires AS to know home CU. spiffe Attack 1: SPIFFE trust-domain is plaintext issuer identity. spiffe Attack 2: WIMSE has no issuer-hiding mechanism. Each baseline was tried and failed on at least one structural axis the construction covers.

formal_security_argument.justification: Attacks reference a defined IND-ISS game, named assumptions (PLONK ZK, Poseidon CR, DLP on Baby Jubjub), and reduction sketches (IND-ISS from PLONK ZK, UNF-CRED from knowledge soundness). However, cryptographer found four gaps all outside threat model: (1) IND-ISS does not cover distributional/semantic leakage from attribute-issuer correlation, (2) SRS honesty assumption not formalized — reduction silently inherits it, (3) 32-gate Boolean evaluator soundness/completeness unproven — breaks UNF-CRED reduction, (4) static IND-ISS game does not compose to multi-proof unlinkability across Merkle root epochs. Threat model stated but reductions have real holes.

implementability.justification: Construction maps to Bolyra primitives (Poseidon hash, PLONK circuit, BabyJubjub EdDSA, Merkle tree, nullifier scheme) with a stated ~42K constraint count. However, auth0_pm Attack 1 identifies no wall-clock proving time on representative hardware (Chromebook at teller, mobile device). Constraint count addresses verifier time but not prover time. Additionally, the 32-gate Boolean evaluator's expressiveness ceiling (no date arithmetic, string set membership, or dynamic schema) means real deployment schemas may exceed the circuit's capabilities without recompilation (spiffe Attack 3, auth0_pm Attack 4).

adversarial_survival.justification: Multiple Tier 3 attacks the construction cannot answer: (1) auth0_pm Attack 3 — BSA/AML auditability paradox is called a 'category blocker'; issuer-hiding is structurally incompatible with 31 U.S.C. § 5318(h) and FinCEN CDD requirements at the named deployment target. (2) cu_ciso Attack 3 — GLBA § 314.4(a) data provenance requirement makes issuer-hiding a regulatory finding, not a feature. (3) cryptographer Attack 2 — subverted SRS collapses IND-ISS completely with no mitigation specified. (4) cu_ciso Attack 2 — revocation latency violates NCUA Part 748 § 748.1(c) with no revocation SLA or mechanism specified. (5) cryptographer Attack 1 — semantic attribute-issuer correlation breaks IND-ISS in practice. The construction survives some attacks (spiffe 1&2, rfc7662 3) but cannot answer the regulatory and SRS attacks.

scenario_fit.justification: CO-OP Shared Branch is a concrete deployment with named stakeholders (Mountain America, Navy Federal) and a real regulatory requirement (NCUA membership verification). However, cu_ciso Attacks 2&3 and auth0_pm Attack 3 demonstrate that the construction's core property (issuer-hiding) is architecturally incompatible with the regulatory obligations at the named deployment (BSA audit trail, GLBA data provenance, NCUA Part 748 incident response). The scenario is concrete but the construction is self-defeating in it without a compliance escape hatch (dual-channel disclosure, audit escrow) that is not specified.

gaps: BSA/AML category blocker: must define a dual-channel disclosure protocol (issuer hidden from verifier but disclosed to regulated audit escrow / CUSO) to satisfy 31 U.S.C. § 5318(h) and GLBA § 314.4(a) at NCUA-examined institutions
SRS honesty assumption must be formalized: specify verifiable MPC ceremony with n≥1 honest participant sufficiency, or switch to transparent proof system (FRI-based STARK / Halo2 IPA)
Extend IND-ISS to distributional variant accounting for attribute-issuer correlation and auxiliary-input adversary with issuer population statistics
Add multi-proof unlinkability game (Camenisch-Lysyanskaya style) with reduction to IND-ISS under root-update composability, or specify epoch-pinning with minimum anonymity set parameter k
Provide wall-clock prover benchmarks on representative hardware (Chromebook, mobile) with SLA commitment or mitigation path (server-side proving with TEE)
Specify issuer registry governance: write authority, update cadence, revocation SLA, availability guarantee, and examiner-readable audit log of issuer key lifecycle

Scope 'arbitrary-schema' claim honestly: define schema as versioned artifact, prove constant-size for bounded attribute count N, table constraint growth, and acknowledge schema evolution requires recompilation
Specify member key custody architecture mapped to FFIEC Authentication Guidance with recovery/re-issuance procedure
